source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Air flows into a heat engine at ambient conditions of 100 kPa, 300 K. Energy is supplied as 1200 kJ per kilogram of air from a 1500 K source, and in some part of the process a heat transfer loss of 300 kJ/kg air occurs at 750 K. The air leaves the engine at 100 kPa, 800 K. Find the second-law efficiencies.

The air leaves the engine at 100 kPa, 800 K. Find the second-law efficiencies.
